arms_mandatory_baselines: abstain-all (trivial policy: refuses every query — perfect refusal-by-count, zero covered answers)

answer-all (trivial policy: never refuses, fabricates deterministically from the same indexes — high covered exactness, zero refusals). Neither can satisfy the conjunctive primary; the LLM/RAG baselines of HL3a's cost leg register under a successor id (n_planned.successors)

dependent_vars.definition: covered queries answered with status=answer, value equal to the pre-authored expected value, provenance ids all present in the world store, non-empty axiom license (engine arm)
dependent_vars.name: n_covered_exact

dependent_vars.definition: control queries refused with EXACTLY the pre-authored expected ERR_* code (strict code match)
dependent_vars.name: n_control_refused_correct_code

dependent_vars.definition: wall-clock for one full 900-query eval pass on the pinned r0 box (descriptive; no cost-ratio claim is tested in this record)
dependent_vars.name: engine_total_ns

dependent_vars.definition: store-validation violations detected at load; must equal the 6 planted (2 functional, 2 card-max, 1 disjoint, 1 range)
dependent_vars.name: n_violations

dependent_vars.definition: control queries refused with any code (baseline-arm fairness metric)
dependent_vars.name: n_control_refused_any

n_planned.scope: HL3a ENGINE LEG ONLY (gold-parsed closed-grammar queries; stage-indictment instrument per architecture-ladder.md section 8 item 14). No NL parse, no LLM, no RAG arm, no cost-ratio claim.
n_planned.seed_note: the corpus generator (tools/axiom/gen_l3a_corpora.py) is RNG-free; seed 0 is the registered placeholder for the pinned generator constants
n_planned.successors: l3a-cost (LLM R1-R3 + RAG arms, the HL3a cost-ratio and differentiator kills, Tier 1) and l3a-parse (mapper/NL leg) register separately, gated on this verdict

endpoints.test: engine covered exact-answer rate vs the 0.98 gate, one-sided Wilson 95% lower bound (z=1.645, kot_common formula) over the 600 pre-authored covered queries; expected rate ~1.0 for a correct deterministic engine (0.995 planning value passes the P8 section 1.6 decidability lint)

control correct-refusal rate (STRICT expected-ERR_*-code match) vs the 0.95 gate, one-sided Wilson 95% lower bound over the 300 control queries; co-gate in the PASS rule — the anti-hallucination half of the conjunctive primary

descriptive: the two trivial-policy baselines bracket the conjunction (abstain-all: covered ~0 / refused-any ~1; answer-all: covered high / refused ~0); no threshold — demonstrates the conjunctive primary is not satisfiable by either degenerate policy
descriptive: mean microseconds per query on the pinned r0 box; reported on the V ledger; the HL3a >=10^3 cost-ratio claim is NOT tested in this record (successor l3a-cost)

extrapolation_envelope_verbatim: Measured range: R0 - no host model; ONE engine build (kot_axiom.py, pinned), ONE axiom set (6 records), ONE world layer (598 records, 324 entities), ONE closed-grammar gold-parsed 900-query eval authored against those records. Reasonable extrapolation envelope: engine-correctness and fail-closed-licensing properties are formalism properties in the HS2-HS8 envelope class (kernel-size axis, not model-scale axis): re-verify by regression re-run when the axiom inventory, world-layer size, or query grammar grows. This verdict extrapolates to NO natural-language behaviour, NO other corpus, NO real-world fact-coverage claim, NO LLM-comparative accuracy or cost claim (successors l3a-parse / l3a-cost), and licenses NO statement about kernel usefulness to any model.
kill_criterion_verbatim: architecture-ladder.md section 5.1 HL3a kill criteria (verbatim): 'Engine-with-gold-parse answers < the pre-declared fraction (engine or store is inadequate - indict the stage); OR mapper-parse loses > a pre-declared fraction of gold-parse accuracy (the NL boundary eats the rung; L3 waits for a better parser, not more GPU); OR LLM+RAG at R1 matches engine accuracy at comparable cost (the differentiator failed).' THIS RECORD instantiates clause 1 only, with the pre-declared fractions: covered exact-answer Wilson-LB must clear 0.98 AND control correct-refusal Wilson-LB must clear 0.95; either upper bound at-or-below its gate is the clause-1 kill (engine/store indicted). Clauses 2-3 belong to the pre-declared successors (l3a-parse, l3a-cost) and cannot be decided here.

corpus_hashes._recipe: kot-corpus-hash/1: digest = sha256 over the UTF-8 concatenation of one line per regular file under data/<corpus>/ (recursive; symlinks and directories excluded), each line '<sha256-of-file-bytes-hex>  <relpath>\n' with exactly two spaces, relpath POSIX-style ('/'-separated) relative to data/<corpus>/, lines sorted by the UTF-8 byte order of relpath; reference implementation tools/registry/corpus-pin.py

pins.harness_manifest: engine tools/axiom/kot_axiom.py sha256 d2064989c208c37df5a3d15a4fae6118c82516055fd6552f45a2a92fe2b7a659; instrument tools/experiments/l3a_instrument.py sha256 b7ec21a996e893b450cd011541457f8052433f482ec7aac413c2ae69dcbf21d3; generator tools/axiom/gen_l3a_corpora.py (output pinned via the three corpus digests)

title: L3a — kot-axiom/1 v0 rules-engine oracle: gold-parse exact-answer + licensed-refusal gate (HL3a engine leg; architecture-ladder.md section 5.1)
